page: Vultr vc2-16c-64gb is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the Cloud Compute family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring an Intel Xeon processor with 16 shared vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 16 MB of L3 cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. The hardware configuration is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a 1280 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

Performance benchmarks place this server consistently in the average tier across most standard operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is measured at 1451.59, while multi-core performance reaches 21535.52. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block speed of 1518.77 GB/sec and a large block read speed of 107.12 GB/sec, alongside a write speed of 53.71 GB/sec. The PassMark CPU score is 21511.0, with database operations registering at 4649.2 and Redis SET operations achieving 23.04 million operations per second. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in single-threaded gzip compression, which registers in the weak tier at 23.39 GB/sec, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at an average rate of 66.52 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation, the vc2-16c-64gb offers a cost-effective option for workloads requiring high resource density in memory and storage without requiring dedicated CPU cores. The combination of 64.0 GB of RAM and a 1280 GB NVMe SSD makes it highly suitable for general-purpose applications, static web serving (benchmarked at 12.01 GB/sec), medium-sized database hosting, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that can tolerate shared CPU scheduling.
description: The Vultr vc2-16c-64gb is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server from the Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 shared vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and a bundled 1280 GB local NVMe SSD, with no GPU hardware. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most metrics, including a PassMark CPU score of 21511.0, Redis SET operations at 23.04 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput of 12.01 GB/sec. A performance tradeoff is present in single-threaded gzip compression, which falls into the weak tier. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for resource-dense workloads, making it well-suited for general-purpose web serving, database hosting, and multi-threaded processing tasks that do not require dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr vc2-16c-64gb is a Cloud Compute virtual server featuring 16 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and a 1280 GB NVMe SSD, delivering balanced general-purpose performance.
meta_description: Vultr vc2-16c-64gb is a general-purpose virtual server with 16 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 1280 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A balanced virtual server featuring sixteen shared vCPUs, sixty-four gigabytes of memory, and a high-capacity local NVMe SSD for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory.
Bundled 1280 GB local NVMe SSD for high-capacity storage.
Average performance in database operations and Redis SET transactions.
Weak single-threaded gzip compression performance as a tradeoff.
Best suited for static web serving, database hosting, and general-purpose applications.
